title: L'Hôpital's rule: ∞/∞
description: Find limits at infinity using L'Hopital's rule. For example, find the limit at infinity of eˣ/x².
topic_path: Using L’Hôpital’s rule for finding limits of indeterminate forms

hints:
$\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} 3x^2=\infty$ and $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} e^x=\infty$, so $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{3x^2}{e^x}$ results in the indeterminate form $\dfrac{\infty}{\infty}$.

We should use l’Hôpital’s rule. [[☃ explanation 1]]
In conclusion, $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{3x^2}{e^x}=0$.

$\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} 4x^2-7x=\infty$ and $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} (\ln(x))^2=\infty$, so $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{4x^2-7x}{(\ln(x))^2}$ results in the indeterminate form $\dfrac{\infty}{\infty}$.

We should use l’Hôpital’s rule. [[☃ explanation 1]]
In conclusion, $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{4x^2-7x}{(\ln(x))^2}=\infty$.

$\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} e^{x^2}=\infty$ and $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} x^4=\infty$, so $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{e^{x^2}}{x^4}$ results in the indeterminate form $\dfrac{\infty}{\infty}$.

We should use l’Hôpital’s rule. [[☃ explanation 1]]
In conclusion, $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{e^{x^2}}{x^4}=\infty$.

$\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} 5x^2-8=\infty$ and $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} 7x-4x^2=-\infty$, so $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{5x^2-8}{7x-4x^2}$ results in the indeterminate form $\dfrac{\infty}{-\infty}$.

We should use l’Hôpital’s rule. [[☃ explanation 1]]
In conclusion, $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{5x^2-8}{7x-4x^2}=-\dfrac{5}{4}$.

$\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} \ln(3x)=\infty$ and $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} e^{7x}=\infty$, so $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{\ln(3x)}{e^{7x}}$ results in the indeterminate form $\dfrac{\infty}{\infty}$.

We should use l’Hôpital’s rule. [[☃ explanation 1]]
In conclusion, $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{\ln(3x)}{e^{7x}}=0$.

$\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} 6x=\infty$ and $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} 15x-8=\infty$, so $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{6x}{15x-8}$ results in the indeterminate form $\dfrac{\infty}{\infty}$.

We should use l’Hôpital’s rule. [[☃ explanation 1]]
In conclusion, $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{6x}{15x-8}=0.4$.

$\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} -3x^3+2x=-\infty$ and $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} 4x+8=\infty$, so $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{-3x^3+2x}{4x+8}$ results in the indeterminate form $\dfrac{-\infty}{\infty}$.

We should use l’Hôpital’s rule. [[☃ explanation 1]]
In conclusion, $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{-3x^3+2x}{4x+8}=-\infty$.

$\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} \ln(x^2)=\infty$ and $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} (\ln(x))^2=\infty$, so $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{\ln(x^2)}{(\ln(x))^2}$ results in the indeterminate form $\dfrac{\infty}{\infty}$.

We should use l’Hôpital’s rule. [[☃ explanation 1]]
In conclusion, $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{\ln(x^2)}{(\ln(x))^2}=0$.

$\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} x^4=\infty$ and $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} 3x^2-7x=\infty$, so $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{x^4}{3x^2-7x}$ results in the indeterminate form $\dfrac{\infty}{\infty}$.

We should use l’Hôpital’s rule. [[☃ explanation 1]]
In conclusion, $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{x^4}{3x^2-7x}=\infty$.

$\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} 8-3x=-\infty$ and $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} 9x^2+11=\infty$, so $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{8-3x}{9x^2+11}$ results in the indeterminate form $\dfrac{-\infty}{\infty}$.

We should use l’Hôpital’s rule. [[☃ explanation 1]]
In conclusion, $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{8-3x}{9x^2+11}=0$.

$\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} e^{5x-3}=\infty$ and $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} \ln(x-2)=\infty$, so $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{e^{5x-3}}{\ln(x-2)}$ results in the indeterminate form $\dfrac{\infty}{\infty}$.

We should use l’Hôpital’s rule. [[☃ explanation 1]]
In conclusion, $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{e^{5x-3}}{\ln(x-2)}=\infty$

$\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} (\ln(6x))^2=\infty$ and $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} (\ln(2x))^2=\infty$, so $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{(\ln(6x))^2}{(\ln(2x))^2}$ results in the indeterminate form $\dfrac{\infty}{\infty}$.

We should use l’Hôpital’s rule. [[☃ explanation 1]]
In conclusion, $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{(\ln(6x))^2}{(\ln(2x))^2}=1$.

$\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} 5x-2x^2=-\infty$ and $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} e^{x+3}=\infty$, so $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{5x-2x^2}{e^{x+3}}$ results in the indeterminate form $\dfrac{-\infty}{\infty}$.

We should use l’Hôpital’s rule. [[☃ explanation 1]]
In conclusion, $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{5x-2x^2}{e^{x+3}}=0$.

$\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} (\ln(x))^3=\infty$ and $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} \ln(4x)=\infty$, so $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{(\ln(x))^3}{\ln(4x)}$ results in the indeterminate form $\dfrac{\infty}{\infty}$.

We should use l’Hôpital’s rule. [[☃ explanation 1]]
In conclusion, $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{(\ln(x))^3}{\ln(4x)}=\infty$.

$\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} 7x^2=\infty$ and $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} x-x^3=-\infty$, so $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{7x^2}{x-x^3}$ results in the indeterminate form $\dfrac{\infty}{-\infty}$.

We should use l’Hôpital’s rule. [[☃ explanation 1]]
In conclusion, $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{7x^2}{x-x^3}=0$.

$\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} 2x^2+5=\infty$ and $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty} 6x^2-9=\infty$, so $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{2x^2+5}{6x^2-9}$ results in the indeterminate form $\dfrac{\infty}{\infty}$.

We should use l’Hôpital’s rule. [[☃ explanation 1]]
In conclusion, $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\dfrac{2x^2+5}{6x^2-9}=\dfrac{1}{3}$.
